purpose: Generator-backed finite graph fixture spec for surfaceology-style crossing graphs. The checker enumerates all simple undirected graphs on 1..4 vertices, derives u_v + product_{w adjacent v} u_w = 1 equations, and checks binary witnesses induced by maximal independent sets.

generator_scope.graph_model: simple undirected labeled graph used only as finite crossing-graph fixture abstraction

equation_template: u_v + product_{w adjacent to v} u_w = 1, with empty product equal to 1.
witness_rule: For every maximal independent set S, set u_v = 0 for v in S and u_v = 1 otherwise. Independence makes every zero vertex see all-neighbor product 1; maximality makes every one vertex have at least one zero neighbor, so product 0.
required_witness_features: at least one graph with an isolated vertex
at least one graph with maximum degree at least 3
at least one graph with more than one maximal independent set
non_promotion_boundary: This generated fixture family checks only labeled simple graphs on 1..4 vertices as finite crossing-graph abstractions. It is not a surfaceology theorem, not a general positive-region solver, not Formation Medium substrate evidence, not a physical settling law, and not engineering readiness.
